scope: Direct-call quick reference for the two Tencent Video main interfaces, optimized for callers who need the current best-known parameter contract rather than the full closure ledger.
caution: This file summarizes the current best direct-call contract in the tested anonymous direct-call scope plus the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay where noted.
It is not proof that aged-cookie or login-state environments behave the same.
When a field is marked partially_closed or environment_open, keep the canonical values unless you are deliberately probing that branch.

current_best_use_cases: Batch CID lookup via one comma-separated idlist key

parameters.name: tid
parameters.shape_or_notes: single query key; repeated tid currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested anonymous branch

failure_signals.errormsg: 閿欒鐨則id, 濡傛灉鏂扮敵璇风殑鎺ュ彛,璇风瓑寰?0鍒嗛挓

parameters.current_best_reading: Primary routing selector. 431 is the stable canonical positive branch. 453 is a separate positive cover-shell-only branch: it returns a non-empty cover shell across 3 public CIDs but still no video_ids, so it is not a field-equivalent replacement for 431. 483 is a thinner success shell that currently materializes video_ids while cover title/type stay empty. 476, 506, and 537 currently stay in the success-shell-without-sample family on the 3-CID matrix.
parameters.environment_scope: same-day anonymous matrix plus the extended 3-CID caller-facing richness matrix and the focused 446-455 / 456-521 follow-ups
still_open: Whether API1 has another positive or success-shell tid beyond the current 431/453/476/483/506/537 family map remains unclosed.

parameters.name: idlist
parameters.shape_or_notes: one comma-separated CID list; duplicate items are preserved; empty slots are ignored when surrounded by valid values; repeated same-name query keys currently first-value-win in the tested same-day anonymous plus real anonymous visitor-cookie replay collision cases

failure_signals.errormsg: idlist个数错误, 为0或超过限制

parameters.current_best_reading: Use one idlist key with CSV payload. The tested clean positive upper bound is 32 non-empty items, and repeated same-name keys do not merge across query-string positions in the tested anonymous collision cases.
parameters.environment_scope: same-day anonymous matrix plus real anonymous visitor-cookie replay collision cases
still_open: Aged-cookie/login-state replay is still open for this row.

parameters.name: appid
parameters.shape_or_notes: canonical numeric appid activates the stricter documented appkey branch; missing/text-like values currently land on a looser tested anonymous branch; the new numeric-focus probe shows a three-family split rather than a simple canonical-vs-other dichotomy

failure_signals.when: tested near-canonical neighbors such as 10001001-10001004 and 10001006-10001010 with the current canonical appkey

parameters.current_best_reading: For stable direct calls, keep the canonical numeric appid. In the tested anonymous numeric slice, appid behavior is now at least three-way: `1` still rides the loose success-bypass branch, a wider tested lower band `10000990-10001000` returns `appid no find`, and the tested near-canonical ranges `10001001-10001004` plus `10001006-10001186` fall into a stricter `appkey error` family instead of `appid no find`, with `10001005` currently acting as the isolated success point inside that scanned region. The current upper boundary search then flips back at `10001187 -> 10010108 / appid no find`.
still_open: Full numeric appid space is still not closed; the new evidence now tightens it to explicit tested cutovers `10001000 -> 10001001` and `10001186 -> 10001187`, but not to a global numeric-space proof outside those scanned regions.

parameters.name: appkey
tested_positive_values: omitted when appid is missing/text in the tested anonymous branch
parameters.shape_or_notes: matters only when the canonical numeric appid branch is active
parameters.current_best_reading: Treat this as part of the canonical documented pair with appid=10001005. The new numeric-focus, contiguous-band, and boundary-search probes also show that nearby tested numeric appids such as `10001004`, and more broadly the scanned noncanonical ranges `10001001-10001004` plus `10001006-10001186`, stay on the same `10010110 / appkey error` branch whether appkey is canonical, missing, or explicitly wrong, so the current canonical appkey does not satisfy that tested near-canonical family.
still_open: No evidence yet that appkey unlocks more than satisfying the exact canonical numeric-appid branch; the tested near-canonical numeric family still rejects the current canonical appkey up to the searched upper edge `10001186`.

current_best_use_cases: VID -> single detail row with duration/defn/state/upload_src
VID batch lookup via one comma-separated idlist key
JSONP wrapper mode when a caller explicitly needs JSONP

parameters.name: otype
parameters.shape_or_notes: only exact lowercase json switches the branch into JSONP; repeated otype currently behaves as first-value-wins in tested anonymous branches
parameters.current_best_reading: Leave it omitted or xml for normal callers. Use exact lowercase json only when you intentionally want JSONP.
still_open: No positive wrapper value is confirmed beyond XML default and lowercase JSONP.

parameters.name: tid
parameters.shape_or_notes: single query key; repeated tid currently behaves as first-value-wins in tested anonymous branches

failure_signals.errormsg: 閿欒鐨則id, 濡傛灉鏂扮敵璇风殑鎺ュ彛,璇风瓑寰?0鍒嗛挓

parameters.current_best_reading: 535 is still the best default when you expect a fuller row. 502 is a richer alternate positive shell with duration+vid+cover_list, but still not a field-equivalent replacement for 535. 488, 540, and 541 currently behave like thinner title/url-led shells on the 3-public-VID matrix. 506 currently stays below the positive-detail threshold and is better read as a success shell.
parameters.environment_scope: same-day anonymous matrix plus the extended 3-VID caller-facing richness matrix and the focused 456-521 follow-up
still_open: Whether API2 has another positive or success-shell tid outside the current 488/502/506/535/540/541 family map remains unclosed.

parameters.name: appid
parameters.shape_or_notes: canonical numeric appid activates the stricter documented appkey branch; missing/text-like values currently land on a looser tested anonymous branch; the new numeric-focus probe shows a three-family split rather than a simple canonical-vs-other dichotomy

failure_signals.when: tested near-canonical neighbors such as 20001233-20001237 and 20001239-20001243 with the current canonical appkey

parameters.current_best_reading: For stable direct calls, keep the canonical numeric appid. In the tested anonymous numeric slice, appid behavior is now at least three-way: `1` still rides the loose success-bypass branch, the lower boundary search finds `20001000 -> appid no find` and `20001001 -> appkey error`, and the upper boundary search finds `20002581 -> appkey error` before flipping back at `20002582 -> appid no find`, with `20001238` currently acting as the isolated success point inside that searched region.
still_open: Full numeric appid space is still not closed; the new evidence now tightens it to explicit tested cutovers `20001000 -> 20001001` and `20002581 -> 20002582`, but not to a global numeric-space proof outside those searched regions.

parameters.name: appkey
tested_positive_values: omitted when appid is missing/text in the tested anonymous branch
parameters.shape_or_notes: matters only when the canonical numeric appid branch is active
parameters.current_best_reading: Treat this as part of the canonical documented pair with appid=20001238. The new numeric-focus, contiguous-band, and boundary-search probes also show that nearby tested numeric appids such as `20001237`, and more broadly the searched noncanonical ranges `20001001-20001237` plus `20001239-20002581`, stay on the same `10010110 / appkey error` branch whether appkey is canonical, missing, or explicitly wrong, so the current canonical appkey does not satisfy that tested near-canonical family.
still_open: No evidence yet that appkey unlocks more than satisfying the exact canonical numeric-appid branch; the tested near-canonical numeric family still rejects the current canonical appkey across the searched ranges `20001001-20001237` and `20001239-20002581`.

parameters.name: union_platform
parameters.shape_or_notes: present in canonical examples; repeated union_platform showed no observed order effect in the tested XML canonical branch under same-day anonymous and real anonymous visitor-cookie replay, and a later anonymous `535/540/541 x union_platform` sweep did not change the richer-vs-thinner field shape on the tested public vids
parameters.current_best_reading: Keep 3 for canonical calls. Current evidence says changing or omitting union_platform shows no visible split in anonymous same-day runs, in the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay, and in the tested `535/540/541 x union_platform` sweep on `z4102qfi0x4` and `j4101ouc4ve`. That is still scoped-negative evidence, not a global no-op proof.
parameters.environment_scope: same-day anonymous matrix plus real anonymous visitor-cookie replay plus the tested anonymous `535/540/541 x union_platform` capability matrix
still_open: Whether union_platform matters under aged-cookie or real login-state replay remains open.
Whether union_platform matters on broader untested public vids remains open, even though the current anonymous `535/540/541` sweep shows no structural enrichment.

auxiliary_or_candidate_parameters.name: callback
auxiliary_or_candidate_parameters.recommended_use: Only with otype=json, when you explicitly need a custom JSONP wrapper.
auxiliary_or_candidate_parameters.current_best_reading: Many values raw-passthrough into the wrapper prefix. Empty callback falls back to the default QZOutputJson shell. Repeated callback now behaves as first-value-wins in the tested same-day anonymous JSONP collision cases, and wrong-appkey JSONP error shells still honor callback wrapper changes. Known parse-breaking representatives now include callback=a(, callback=((, callback=})();, and the later widened unmatched-delimiter / odd-prefix representatives callback=[(, callback={(, callback=/*(, callback=a[(, callback=)(, callback=](, and callback=}(, while callback=[ and callback=[[ still remain raw-passthrough in the tested branch.
auxiliary_or_candidate_parameters.environment_scope: same-day anonymous matrix; callback precedence and wrong-appkey error-shell behavior also replay-stable in the tested real anonymous visitor-cookie PC Web and Mobile H5 environments
still_open: Aged-cookie/login-state replay is still open for callback precedence and error-shell behavior.
Broader parser-breaking families remain open beyond the newly widened unmatched-delimiter / odd-prefix representatives, but they stay lower priority than the environment gap above.

auxiliary_or_candidate_parameters.current_best_reading: No observed effect in the tested anonymous canonical branches or the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay on API1 tid=431 and API2 tid family 535/540/541.
auxiliary_or_candidate_parameters.environment_scope: anonymous same-day matrix plus real anonymous visitor-cookie replay
still_open: Whether any auth-leaning key only matters after login-state gating
